title: Documentation
description: How to add new screens and pages using the HAI3 framework.
quick_start_desc: Use the frontx CLI to scaffold a new screenset with a default screen:
structure_desc: Each screenset follows a strict vertical-slice architecture:
structure_tree:
src/screensets/{name}/
  {name}Screenset.tsx    — config & registration
  ids.ts                — all IDs in one place
  i18n/                 — screenset-level translations (36 langs)
  screens/              — one folder per screen
    {screen}/
      {Screen}Screen.tsx
      i18n/             — screen-level translations
      components/       — screen-local components
  actions/              — event-emitting actions
  effects/              — event-to-slice bridges
  events/               — domain event definitions
  slices/               — Redux state slices
  api/                  — API services with mocks
  uikit/                — screenset-local UI components
step1_text: Add a screen ID constant in ids.ts (e.g. export const MY_SCREEN_ID = 'myScreen').
step2_text: Create the screen folder: screens/myScreen/ with MyScreenScreen.tsx and i18n/ folder with all 36 language JSON files.
step3_text: Register the screen in the screenset config menu array inside {name}Screenset.tsx with a lazy import.
step4_text: Add screenset-level i18n entries for the menu label in all 36 language files.
step5_text: Run validation: npm run type-check && npm run arch:check && npm run lint.
rule_uikit: Always use @hai3/uikit components first; create local uikit only if a component is missing.
rule_events: Follow event-driven architecture: Component -> Action -> Event -> Effect -> Slice -> Store.
rule_i18n: All text must use t() with proper namespaces. No hardcoded strings.
rule_no_inline: No inline component definitions in Screen files. Decompose into components/ first.
commands_desc: Use these commands in your AI assistant to scaffold, validate, and manage HAI3 code.
cmd_new_screenset_desc: Create a new screenset from scratch via frontx CLI. Gathers name, category, and initial screens, then generates full vertical slice with events, slices, API, and i18n.
cmd_duplicate_screenset_desc: Duplicate an existing screenset. Copies all files and updates ids.ts only — everything else auto-updates via template literals.
cmd_new_screen_desc: Add a new screen to an existing screenset. Creates screen component, i18n files for all 36 languages, and optionally adds menu entry.
cmd_new_component_desc: Add a new UI component. Checks @hai3/uikit first; creates screenset-local base or composite component only if missing.
cmd_new_action_desc: Create a new action following the event-driven flux pattern: Action -> Event -> Effect -> Slice. Generates event definition, action, and effect files.
cmd_new_api_service_desc: Add a new domain-based API service with mocks. Creates service, events, actions, slices, and effects with full event-driven data flow.
cmd_validate_desc: Run full validation: arch:check, type-check, lint, and common violation checks. Reports results and suggests fixes.
cmd_fix_violation_desc: Fix a specific rule violation. Identifies the violated rule, routes to the correct target file, applies the fix, and verifies.
cmd_review_pr_desc: Comprehensive code review against HAI3 rules. Checks event-driven architecture, imports, types, styling, registry, and API rules. Outputs verdict: Approve / Request Changes / Comment.
cmd_rules_desc: Show HAI3 rules for a specific area. Routes to the correct target file and summarizes critical rules, stop conditions, and common violations.
cmd_quick_ref_desc: Quick reference card for event-driven flow, imports, components, styling, i18n, and invariants.
cmd_arch_explain_desc: Explain HAI3 architecture concepts with diagrams and code examples. Covers event-driven flow, registry pattern, three-layer architecture, and more.
cmd_update_guidelines_desc: Update custom company or project guidelines. Modifies only .ai/company/ or .ai/project/ files — never touches HAI3 base guidelines.
